questions.question: If FRIEND is coded as HUMJTK, how is CANDLE coded?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by 2 positions: C→E, A→D, N→R, D→I, L→R, E→L → EDRIRL.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: 11, 13, 17, 19, 23, 25
questions.explanation: All others are prime numbers. 25 = 5 × 5, so it is not a prime number.

questions.question: If 'A' is south of 'B', 'C' is east of 'B', in which direction is 'C' with respect to 'A'?
questions.explanation: B is north of A. C is east of B. So C is North-East of A.

questions.question: Pointing to a photograph, a man says, 'She is the daughter of the only son of my grandfather.' How is the woman in the photograph related to the man?
questions.explanation: Man's grandfather's only son = man's father. Father's daughter = man's sister.

questions.question: If 6 × 4 = 46, 7 × 3 = 37, then 8 × 5 = ?
questions.explanation: The pattern reverses and combines the two numbers: 6×4 → 46 (reverse of 64), 7×3 → 37 (reverse), 8×5 → 58.

questions.question: In a row of 40 boys, Ravi is 11th from the left. What is his position from the right?
questions.explanation: Position from right = Total – Position from left + 1 = 40 – 11 + 1 = 30.

questions.question: Arrange the following in logical order: 1-Word, 2-Letter, 3-Sentence, 4-Paragraph, 5-Phrase
questions.explanation: Correct order of increasing size: Letter → Word → Phrase → Sentence → Paragraph = 2,1,5,3,4.

questions.question: Find the missing number: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
A clock shows 3:15. What is the angle between the hour and minute hands?
Choose the correct mirror image of the word 'INDIA' when the mirror is placed at its right side:
questions.explanation: The mirror image of INDIA when placed at right reverses the word left-right: AIDNI.

questions.question: If HOSPITAL is coded as 32 (8 letters × 4), and SCHOOL is coded as 24 (6 letters × 4), then COLLEGE = ?
questions.explanation: COLLEGE has 7 letters × 4 = 28.

questions.question: Study the following: 'All cats are dogs. All dogs are horses. Some horses are cows.' Conclusion: All cats are horses.
questions.explanation: All cats are dogs (given). All dogs are horses (given). Therefore, by transitivity, All cats are horses — TRUE.

questions.question: A is the father of B. B is the brother of C. C is the mother of D. How is A related to D?
questions.explanation: A is B's father. B is C's brother, so A is also C's father. C is D's mother. So A is D's maternal grandfather.

questions.question: Which number comes next? 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ?
questions.explanation: This is the Fibonacci sequence — each term = sum of two preceding: 8+13=21.

questions.question: P walks 10 km North, then turns right and walks 5 km, then turns right and walks 10 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.explanation: P goes 10N, 5E, 10S — ends up 5 km East of start. Distance = 5 km.

questions.question: If the day before yesterday was Thursday, what day will be the day after tomorrow?
questions.explanation: Day before yesterday = Thursday → Yesterday = Friday → Today = Saturday → Tomorrow = Sunday → Day after tomorrow = Monday.

questions.question: In a certain code, 'GOLD' is written as 'IQNF'. How is 'WIND' written in that code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted +2: G→I, O→Q, L→N, D→F. So W→Y, I→K, N→P, D→F = YKPF.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Triangle, Square, Cube, Rectangle
questions.explanation: Cube is a 3D figure (solid shape), while Triangle, Square, and Rectangle are 2D figures (plane shapes).

questions.question: A is 3 years older than B, who is twice as old as C. If the total age of A, B, C is 33, how old is B?
Water is related to Thirst as Food is related to:
questions.explanation: Water satisfies Thirst; Food satisfies Hunger — same relationship.

questions.question: If in a certain language 'pik da bol' means 'come to me', 'da mit pe' means 'you go now', 'pe bol ja' means 'now come back', what does 'bol' mean?
questions.explanation: 'bol' appears in 'pik da bol' (come to me) and 'pe bol ja' (now come back). Common meaning = 'come'.

questions.question: How many triangles are there in the following figure? (A large triangle divided into 4 equal smaller triangles)
questions.explanation: 4 small triangles + 1 large triangle = 5 triangles total.

questions.question: Choose the figure that best completes the series: Circle → Semi-circle → Triangle → ?
questions.explanation: Each shape is halved: Full circle → Half circle → Triangle (half of square concept) → Rectangle (half of square).

questions.question: If 'TEACHER' is decoded from 'VGCEJGT', what decoding rule is applied?
questions.explanation: T+2=V, E+2=G, A+2=C, C+2=E, H+2=J, E+2=G, R+2=T → VGCEJGT. Rule: +2 to each letter.

questions.question: A man sold an article for ₹540 at a loss of 10%. At what price should it be sold to gain 10%?
Statements: All pens are pencils. Some pencils are erasers. Conclusion I: Some erasers are pens. II: All pens are erasers.
questions.explanation: From 'All pens are pencils' and 'Some pencils are erasers', we cannot definitively conclude that any pen is an eraser.

questions.question: If PENCIL is written as 4-5-14-3-9-12, then PAPER is written as:
questions.explanation: Letters are coded as their alphabetical positions: P=16, A=1, P=16, E=5, R=18.

questions.question: Arrange in order: Word → Dictionary → Library → Sentence → Paragraph
questions.explanation: Logical order by size: Word → Sentence → Paragraph → Dictionary (contains all) → Library (contains many dictionaries).

questions.question: Look at this series: 7, 10, 8, 11, 9, 12, ... What number should come next?
questions.explanation: Two alternating series: 7,8,9... and 10,11,12,... Next term in first series = 10.

questions.question: In a family, there are 6 members: A, B, C, D, E, F. A and B are a married couple. C is the only son of D and F. D is the brother of A. E is the daughter of B. Who is C's father?
questions.explanation: C is the only son of D and F → D is C's father.
